title: paspalum laeve subsp. circulare
intro: Paspalum laeve subsp. circulare, commonly known as paspalum or dallisgrass, is a type of grass native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of paspalum laeve subsp. circulare can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
